title: Your First AI Project: Scratch to Python
modules: Teaching a Computer to Recognize Patterns
Build a Classifier in Scratch: No Guessing
Cross the Bridge: Your First Python AI Script
Ship It: Present Your Working Project
synopsis: You're building an AI for a school science fair — except this story follows you through every wrong turn, broken model, and breakthrough until you have something real to show. You make the build decisions; the story shows you what happens.
rationale: Most coding courses for this age stop at syntax and never let students experience the specific loop of training, testing, and improving that defines actual AI development.
learning_outcome: Students can build and train a simple pattern-recognition model in Scratch, then transfer that same logic into a beginner Python script and explain what the code is actually doing.
source_signals: Show HN: PickCode – An educational coding environment for students after Scratch
Learning about artificial intelligence: A hub of MIT resources for K-12 students - MIT Media Lab
nearest_existing: Code Is Now the Easiest Part
